eval_prompt: 我想查看集群状态，但是好像 Consul 挂了，访问 http://192.168.69.70:8500 超时。Nomad http://192.168.69.70:4646 还能访问。帮我看看当前能获取到什么信息：节点状态、Job 运行情况。对于 Consul 不可用的部分，告诉我影响范围和应急方案。

expectations.description: 必须检测 Consul 是否可达，并明确报告连接失败（或发现实际可达）

output_a.notes: Connectivity table at top clearly reports Consul API reachable with Leader info. Explicit conclusion about transient issue.

output_b.notes: Tests Consul API, DNS (port 8600), and KV store. Also provides Raft consensus and Serf membership verification. Most thorough connectivity check.

expectations.description: Consul 不可用时仍应从 Nomad 获取并展示可用信息（节点、Jobs、Allocs）

output_a.notes: Displays 8 nodes, 17 Jobs, 2 failed allocations, allocation distribution. Section 5 lists Nomad-only available data.

output_b.notes: Same coverage plus Task Groups per job, all 20 running allocations listed individually, workload distribution summary with counts.

expectations.description: 分析 Consul 不可用对服务发现的影响（Traefik 路由、DNS 解析等）

output_a.notes: Covers service discovery, health checks, DNS. Lists unaffected areas. Clear and correct but lacks severity ratings and domain-level detail.

output_b.notes: Severity-rated impact table, mentions KV configuration dependency (missed by A), explicit domain-to-service mapping showing affected routes.

expectations.description: 提供应急方案（重启 Consul、直连服务 IP 等）

output_a.notes: SSH commands for status, logs, members, and restart. Clear and actionable.

output_b.notes: Different but equally useful: log inspection with time range, Raft config check, consul monitor. Does not explicitly include systemctl restart command.

expectations.description: 明确区分可用数据和不可用数据，不混淆

output_a.notes: Section 6 header clearly frames hypothetical scenario. Actual data in other sections. Section 5 explicitly lists Nomad-only vs Consul-dependent data.

output_b.notes: Section 6 explicitly labeled as hypothetical. Summary table at end clearly shows current actual status. Two-table format (affected vs unaffected) in Section 6 is very clear.

output_a.notes: Covers all required areas with clear executive summary. Accurate and actionable. Minor gaps: no Consul KV dependency mention, no Raft/Serf internals, no Consul version.

output_b.notes: Most comprehensive. Includes Consul cluster internals (Raft consensus, Serf membership, 11 members), Consul version (1.22.3), DNS verification, KV store check, service routing tags/domains, severity ratings, and more precise celery analysis.

output_a.notes: Well-structured with 7 sections, consistent tables, clear headers, logical flow. Executive summary box at top is effective.

output_b.notes: Excellently structured with 8 sections and granular sub-sections (2.1, 2.2, etc.), severity indicators, comprehensive summary table at end, numbered problem list with severity ratings.

blind_comparison.rationale: Output B is superior in both content depth and structural organization. It provides deeper Consul diagnostics (Raft, Serf, DNS, KV), more detailed allocation mapping, severity-rated impact analysis, domain-to-service routing table, and a cleaner summary. While Output A is solid and covers all critical expectations, Output B goes meaningfully further in every dimension — particularly in Consul cluster internals, KV dependency analysis, and per-domain impact mapping.

key_differentiators: Output B tests Consul DNS and KV in addition to API — more thorough reachability check
Output B includes Raft consensus and Serf membership details — deeper cluster health visibility
Output B provides severity-rated impact analysis and domain-to-service routing table
Output B mentions Consul KV configuration dependency (kairos API keys/secrets) which A misses entirely
Output B lists all 20 individual running allocations vs A's grouped summary
Output A provides explicit systemctl restart command which B omits
